name: White Chocolate Mousse/Chocolate Cups
recipeInstructions:
~------------------------------------------CHOCOLATE CUPS:----------------
~---------------------------------------------MOUSSE:
Melt the white chocolate in a microwave until smooth. Using a small pastry brush, coat the entire inside surface of each fluted cup, making sure to get into every pleat. Apply a second or third coat if necessary. Place cups in the freezer to set.
Remove individual cups one at a time and carefully peel off foil, handling gently. If chocolate breaks but foil remains intact, reapply more chocolate and return to freezer until solid.
Keep filled cups in the freezer until ready for mousse.
WHITE CHOCOLATE MOUSSE: Use Tobler Narcisse or Droste White, or any other fine imported white chocolate. Melt in a double boiler, then add warmed cream and stir until smooth. Cool mixture to room temperature. Beat egg whites to stiff peaks.
Mix 1/3 of the whites into the cooled chocolate mix to lighten it, then fold in the remaining whites. Fold whipped cream into the egg white mixture and refrigerate until set. Gently spoon mousse into chocolate cups; garnish with chocolate curls or grated dark chocolate.
